source: Things to do in Raleigh this Weekend! — Thingstodo919

events.name: Durham Bulls vs. Jacksonville
events.tagline: Minor League baseball with Friday fireworks at the ballpark.
events.description: Durham Bulls host Jacksonville at Durham Bulls Athletic Park in Durham. The listing notes Friday fireworks and an admission price starting at $13.

events.name: Max Richter with the American Contemporary Music Ensemble, performing The Blue Notebooks and In A Landscape
events.tagline: Max Richter brings a one-night performance of two signature works to DPAC.
events.address: 123 Vivian Street / William V. “Bill” Bell Way
events.description: Max Richter performs at DPAC in Durham on Friday, June 19, 2026, with the American Contemporary Music Ensemble. The program features The Blue Notebooks and In A Landscape.

events.name: The Official FIFA World Cup Watch Party: USA vs. Australia
events.tagline: Cheer on Team USA with giveaways, custom gear, and a free World Cup watch party at The Rialto Theatre.
events.description: A free FIFA World Cup watch party for the USA vs. Australia match at The Rialto Theatre in Midtown Raleigh. The event includes giveaways and custom event gear, and advance RSVP is required.

events.name: Garrison Keillor
events.tagline: An evening of storytelling, poetry, and sing-alongs with Garrison Keillor.
events.venue: Meymandi Concert Hall at Martin Marietta Center for the Performing Arts
events.description: Garrison Keillor appears at Meymandi Concert Hall at Martin Marietta Center for the Performing Arts in Raleigh on Friday, June 19, 2026 at 7:00 PM. The show was originally scheduled for January 31 and later rescheduled.

events.name: Juneteenth Celebration
events.tagline: A free family celebration of freedom, culture, and community in downtown Raleigh.
events.freeNote: Free admission; tickets required for entry to Marbles Kids Museum
events.description: Join Marbles Kids Museum and Moore Square at the park and on the Playway at Marbles for a Juneteenth celebration with local artists, storytimes, a vendor market, food trucks, games, and music. The event is free to the public, but tickets are required for entry to Marbles Kids Museum.

events.name: Andrew Rudick
events.tagline: A nationally touring stand-up comic brings a run of shows to Room 861 at Goodnights.
events.description: Andrew Rudick is scheduled to perform at Room 861 at Goodnights Comedy Club in Raleigh on Friday, June 19, 2026, at 7:30 PM. Goodnights lists the show as part of a June 19–21 run in Room 861.

events.name: Ralph Barbosa
events.tagline: Ralph Barbosa brings a weekend of stand-up to Goodnights Comedy Club.
events.description: Ralph Barbosa is scheduled for a Friday performance at Goodnights Comedy Club in Raleigh on June 19, 2026, with shows at 7:00 PM and 9:15 PM. The event is a ticketed stand-up comedy appearance at the club's Midtown Raleigh location.

events.name: Kam Patterson
events.tagline: Kam Patterson brings a weekend of stand-up to Raleigh Improv.
events.scheduleNote: Fri 7:00 PM · Fri 9:30 PM · Sat 6:30 PM · Sat 9:30 PM · Sun 6:30 PM
events.description: Kam Patterson is scheduled for a five-show stand-up run at Raleigh Improv in Cary over the June 19–21 weekend. The listing shows performances across Friday, Saturday, and Sunday.

events.name: Babyface Ray X 42 Dugg - 4 The Streets Tour
events.tagline: Babyface Ray and 42 Dugg bring their tour to The Ritz for a Friday night hip-hop show.
events.description: Hip-hop concert featuring Babyface Ray and 42 Dugg at The Ritz in Raleigh. Multiple ticketing sources list the show for Friday, June 19, 2026 at 7:00 PM.

events.name: Music in the Park Juneteenth Celebration Morrisville 2026
events.tagline: Free Juneteenth concert with live music and food trucks at Indian Creek Trailhead.
events.description: Morrisville’s Music in the Park series includes a Juneteenth Celebration at Indian Creek Trailhead on Friday, June 19, 2026. The town lists free live music by Sahara Reggae Band, along with a food truck and vendor market.

events.name: Movie Night – The Parent Trap
events.tagline: Free outdoor screening of the classic family film at Fenton Square.
events.description: An outdoor screening of The Parent Trap at Fenton Square in Cary. The event is listed for Friday, June 19, 2026, from 7–9 PM and is free to attend.

events.name: Square Dance Social at Dix Park
events.tagline: Learn lively square dances outdoors at Dix Park for a free Friday evening social.
events.venue: Gipson Play Plaza at Dix Park
events.description: An outdoor square dance gathering at Dix Park with easy partner dances and no experience needed. The event takes place at Gipson Play Plaza and runs from 6:00 PM to 8:00 PM.

events.name: Juneteenth: A Chavis Celebration
events.tagline: A free Juneteenth day-and-night celebration at historic Chavis Park.
events.venue: John Chavis Memorial Park and Community Center
events.address: 505 Martin Luther King Jr Blvd., Raleigh, NC 27601
events.description: Join the City of Raleigh at John Chavis Memorial Park for a Juneteenth celebration featuring waterslides, water games, arts and crafts, a live DJ, vendor booths, wellness screenings, a splash pad, food trucks, live music, and evening performances. The event honors freedom, resilience, and community.

events.name: Juneteenth Live: Donnie Deneil + Scrumptious w/ The Phoebes, Strangers Until Now
events.tagline: A Juneteenth showcase of local bands at The Pour House.
events.venue: The Pour House Music Hall & Record Shop
events.description: Juneteenth Live features Donnie Deneil and Scrumptious with The Phoebes and Strangers Until Now at The Pour House Music Hall & Record Shop in Raleigh. The event is listed for Friday, June 19, 2026, with doors at 8:00 PM and showtime at 9:00 PM.

events.name: Candlelight: Tribute to Taylor Swift
events.tagline: Taylor Swift’s biggest hits performed live by candlelight in a historic barn setting.
events.description: Candlelight concerts present a live string performance of Taylor Swift songs under candlelight at The Historic Wakefield Barn. The event is scheduled for Friday, June 19, 2026, and runs about 60–75 minutes.

events.name: Candlelight: 90s Unplugged
events.tagline: A candlelit string-quartet tribute to 1990s hits.
events.description: Fever presents a Candlelight concert featuring the music of 90s Unplugged at The Historic Wakefield Barn. The performance is scheduled for Friday, June 19, 2026, with assigned seating and an age minimum of 8 years old.

events.name: ADF Presents: Bill T. Jones/Arnie Zane Company
events.tagline: A landmark modern dance company returns to Durham for ADF’s 2026 season.
events.description: American Dance Festival presents Bill T. Jones/Arnie Zane Company performing at Page Auditorium at Duke in Durham. The program is listed as 70 minutes with no intermission and includes 'Continuous Replay' and 'Story'.

events.name: Towards Zero
events.tagline: Agatha Christie’s classic murder mystery comes to Titmus Theatre for TheatreFEST.
events.description: University Theatre presents Agatha Christie and Gerald Verner’s thriller Towards Zero at Titmus Theatre. The production runs June 11–21, 2026 at NC State’s TheatreFEST.

events.name: Dear Evan Hansen
events.tagline: The Broadway hit comes to Theatre Raleigh for an emotional, limited run.
events.venue: De Ann S. Jones Theatre at Theatre Raleigh Arts Center
events.description: Dear Evan Hansen runs at Theatre Raleigh from June 3–21, 2026. The production is staged at De Ann S. Jones Theatre and tells the story of a high school senior struggling to belong.

events.name: Anastasia the Musical
events.tagline: A sweeping Broadway musical about identity, family, and finding home.
events.scheduleNote: Runs June 5–21, 2026 · Friday showtime not specified in source
events.description: Anastasia the Musical is running at The Justice Theater Project in Raleigh from June 5 through June 21, 2026. The production is a stage adaptation of the animated film, following a young orphan’s search for her past.

events.name: Zebulon Devil Dogz vs. Greenville Yard Gnomes
events.tagline: Friday night Coastal Plain League baseball at Five County Stadium.
events.description: The Zebulon Devil Dogz host the Greenville Yard Gnomes in a Coastal Plain League baseball game at Five County Stadium. The listed game time is 6:30 PM on Friday, June 19, 2026.

events.name: The Road From Emancipation to Freedom
events.tagline: A Juneteenth talk tracing the legal journey from emancipation to freedom.
events.description: Torrey Dixon presents a Juneteenth talk on the legal and historical significance of Juneteenth at the City of Raleigh Museum. The event is presented in partnership with the Capital City Lawyers Association.

events.name: Summer at the Springs Featuring Joe Hero Band (Foo Fighters Tribute)
events.tagline: Free Friday night outdoor concert with Joe Hero’s Foo Fighters tribute set.
events.description: Summer at the Springs is a free outdoor concert series at the Holly Springs Cultural Center. This June 19 event features Joe Hero Band performing Foo Fighters tribute music, with food trucks and beer and wine available for purchase.

events.name: Neem The Animist - Juneteenth Album Release / Cardigan / Tweekeen / Trillville Rasta / Dymira Decadent / Truth Decay
events.tagline: A Juneteenth album release show at KINGS featuring Neem The Animist and a stacked local lineup.
events.description: Neem The Animist presents a Juneteenth album release show at KINGS in Raleigh with Cardigan, Tweekeen, Trillville Rasta, Dymira Decadent, and Truth Decay. The event is listed for Friday, June 19, 2026, with doors at 7:00 PM and admission of $10.

events.name: Necromancy: Goth Night w/ Corpse Dust
events.tagline: A late-night goth dance party with live music from Corpse Dust.
events.description: Necromancy: Goth Night w/ Corpse Dust takes place at Chapel of Bones in Raleigh on Friday, June 19, 2026. VisitRaleigh lists it as an 8pm–1am event at 658 Maywood Ave. Ste. B 110, and the event is 18+ with valid ID.

events.name: Ice Cream Social 2026
events.tagline: Beat the heat with a free scoop and some summer social time.
events.description: A free ice cream social at the Morrisville Senior Center, sponsored by Cornerstone Caregiving. The Town of Morrisville lists it for Friday, June 19, 2026, with registration required.

events.name: Holly Springs Salamanders vs. Wilmington Sharks
events.tagline: College summer baseball with a Friday night first pitch in Holly Springs.
events.description: The Holly Springs Salamanders host the Wilmington Sharks in a Coastal Plain League game at North Main Athletic Complex, listed for Friday, June 19 at 6:30 PM. The matchup is also shown on the team schedule as a home game in Holly Springs.

events.name: Fire of Freedom
events.tagline: A free one-man performance tracing Abraham Galloway’s fight for freedom and citizenship.
events.description: Mike Wiley Productions presents a free performance of Fire of Freedom at the Alston-Massenburg Center. The one-man show tells the story of Abraham Galloway, an enslaved man who escaped to freedom and became a Union spy, abolitionist, and political leader.

events.name: Danger!
events.tagline: A wild solo show about a man obsessed with proving his neighbor’s son is a changeling.
events.venue: Burning Coal Theatre - The Murphey School
events.description: Burning Coal Theatre presents Danger!, a solo play in Raleigh about a man who believes his neighbor’s son is a supernatural being and tries to prove it. It is part of the theatre’s Second Stage series and runs at the Murphey School location.

events.name: An Evening with Folkknot
events.tagline: A Friday night of folk-rock at the Wake Forest Listening Room.
events.description: Folkknot performs at The Wake Forest Listening Room on Friday, June 19, 2026. The event is listed as all ages with doors at 6:45 PM and the show beginning at 7:30 PM.

events.name: Aittala x Embrace Oblivion x Crimson Steel
events.tagline: A heavy metal triple bill brings crushing riffs to Cannonball Music Hall.
events.description: Aittala, Embrace the Oblivion, and Crimson Steel are scheduled to perform at Cannonball Music Hall in Raleigh on Friday, June 19, 2026. Visit Raleigh lists the showtime as 7:30–11pm with paid admission.

events.name: Aaron Hamm and Big River Band; Kaden Jones Band/Tan Sanders
events.tagline: A Friday night country show at Lincoln Theatre.
events.description: Aaron Hamm and Big River Band with Kaden Jones Band/Tan Sanders is scheduled for Friday, June 19, 2026 at Lincoln Theatre in Raleigh. Venue listings show a 7:15 PM show time with doors at 6:15 PM.

events.name: 2026 NASCAR Regional Season - Denny's of Garner 190
events.tagline: Friday night short-track racing at Wake County Speedway.
events.scheduleNote: Fri 7–11 PM; racing starts at 8:30 PM
events.description: NASCAR Regional racing returns to Wake County Speedway for the Denny's of Garner 190. The event takes place on Friday, June 19, 2026, with gates opening before an 8:30 PM green flag.

events.name: The Rocky Horror Picture Show
events.tagline: A late-night cult-film screening with live shadow-cast energy at The Rialto.
events.description: The Low Down Cheap Little Punks present The Rocky Horror Picture Show at The Rialto Theatre in Raleigh. The event is listed for Friday, June 19, 2026, with doors at 11:30 PM and showtime at 11:55 PM.

events.name: Juneteenth at Pinhook
events.tagline: A Juneteenth celebration with late-night energy at The Pinhook.
events.description: Dirty South Juneteenth Celebration Party at The Pinhook is a 18+ club event featuring DJ Sol B2B VER and Sex Party B2B Deyon. The event is presented as a celebration of Southern club culture with a sliding-scale door price.

events.name: Party at Future Hooman Art Exhibit
events.tagline: An interactive 3rd Friday art party with DJs, drinks, and a solstice-themed vibe.
events.description: The Flux & Drift party series at Future Hooman returns during Downtown Durham’s 3rd Friday Art Walk with the themed event 'The Long Night.' The evening includes DJs, an interactive exhibit space, light snacks and drinks, and an optional solstice connection experiment.

events.name: Monét Noelle Marshall
events.tagline: An intimate, immersive one-woman show from Durham-based multidisciplinary artist Monét Noelle Marshall.
events.description: A one-woman performance titled 'Ain't No Such Thing As a One-Woman Show' by Monét Noelle Marshall at Hayti Heritage Center in Durham. The event is scheduled for Friday evening and is part of her Durham-based multidisciplinary work.

events.name: Juneteenth at Hub RTP
events.tagline: A free family-friendly Juneteenth block party with live performances, art, and food in Durham.
events.description: Hub RTP's Juneteenth celebration returns on June 19, 2026, with live performances, interactive art, educational experiences, vendors, and family activities. The event is a free, public gathering at the Horseshoe at Hub RTP in Durham.

events.name: We Set Our Terms: A Black Erotic Praxis Open Mic: Third Friday Juneteenth Edition
events.tagline: A Juneteenth open mic centered on Black erotic expression, consent, and liberation.
events.description: A Juneteenth-themed open mic event in Durham focused on Black erotic praxis, consent, and liberation. The listing places it at Zeitgeist Gallery & Bar on Friday at 5 PM.

events.name: Juneteenth Observance Ceremony
events.tagline: A free Juneteenth gathering at Catawba Trail Farm honoring Black freedom and community.
events.description: This Juneteenth observance ceremony takes place at Catawba Trail Farm in Durham and is hosted by UCAN. The event is listed as a free, preregistered community celebration on Friday afternoon.

events.name: Honoring Juneteenth In Our Courts
events.tagline: Celebrate the trailblazing legacy of Black lawyers in North Carolina while honoring Juneteenth history and freedom.
events.description: This Juneteenth event features a guest panel on Black lawyers' legacy in North Carolina, performances, and entertainment at the NCCU Student Center. Hosted by the NCCU School of Law Social Justice and Racial Equity Institute, the North Carolina Association of Public Defenders, and the George H. White Bar Association.

events.name: Elizabeth June: NOTES from a NARCISSISTIC NEGRO & Other N Words (NNN) One-Woman Show
events.tagline: An award-winning solo show blending humor, songs, and spoken word into a story of survival and healing.
events.description: An autobiographical one-woman show written and performed by Elizabeth June. The performance recounts survival, healing, and triumph through songs, spoken word, stand-up, and stories.

events.name: Chapel Hill-Carrboro Juneteenth Celebration
events.tagline: Celebrate Juneteenth with live performances, local food, and community activities.
events.description: The annual Chapel Hill-Carrboro Juneteenth Celebration is a community gathering at Hargraves Community Center. It includes live performances, history exhibits, art vendors, nonprofits, local food, dancing, and activities for children.

events.name: Carolina Hurricanes Championship Celebration Parade
events.tagline: The Stanley Cup is back in Raleigh with a massive victory parade and fan rally.
events.scheduleNote: Sat 11:00 AM · Parade ends at Davie St. with rally at City Plaza
events.description: The Carolina Hurricanes will celebrate their 2026 Stanley Cup victory with a parade starting at 11:00 a.m. in Downtown Raleigh, followed by a fan rally at City Plaza.

events.name: Hank Williams Jr.
events.tagline: Outlaw country legend Hank Williams Jr. brings his 2026 tour to Raleigh.
events.venue: Coastal Credit Union Music Park at Walnut Creek
events.description: Hank Williams Jr. performs at Coastal Credit Union Music Park at Walnut Creek in Raleigh on Saturday, June 20, 2026. The concert is scheduled for 6:30 PM and tickets start at $43+.

events.name: Carolina Flyers Ultimate Frisbee vs Vegas Bighorns — Narwhal Night
events.tagline: The Flyers’ annual Narwhal Night brings pro ultimate to Durham County Memorial Stadium.
events.description: Professional ultimate frisbee matchup featuring the Carolina Flyers against the Vegas Bighorns at Durham County Memorial Stadium. The event is billed as Narwhal Night and is scheduled for Saturday, June 20, 2026 at 7:30 PM.

events.name: Capital City Juneteenth Celebration
events.tagline: A free afternoon of Juneteenth reflection, live entertainment, food, and family activities at Dix Park.
events.description: The community celebration takes place at Harvey Hill in Dorothea Dix Park on Saturday, June 20, 2026, from 2:00 to 6:30 PM. It features entertainment, kids activities, vendors, and food in honor of Juneteenth.

events.name: Juneteenth Celebration 2026
events.tagline: A free Juneteenth celebration with live performances and cultural programming at Downtown Cary Park.
events.description: Cary’s annual Juneteenth celebration takes place at Downtown Cary Park on Saturday, June 20, 2026, from 4 to 9:30 p.m. The event features live performances, spoken word, dance, and interactive activities curated by Historian Darrell Stover.

events.name: Apex Juneteenth Festival
events.tagline: A free community celebration of Juneteenth with music, food, and local vendors.
events.description: Juneteenth in the Peak commemorates the end of slavery in the United States through education, culture, and entertainment. The event features live music and performances, food vendors, Juneteenth storytelling, and local vendors at Town Hall Campus.

events.name: Venetian Vespers Music
events.tagline: An early-music Vespers concert of Monteverdi and Carissimi in Duke Chapel.
events.description: This concert features larger-scale works with soloists and period instruments in the format of a traditional Vespers evening prayer service. The program includes psalm settings by Claudio Monteverdi and concludes with Giacomo Carissimi's eight-part Magnificat.

events.name: Mozart by Moonlight at Summerfest
events.tagline: An evening of Mozart under the stars with the North Carolina Symphony.
events.description: UNC Health Summerfest presents Mozart by Moonlight with the North Carolina Symphony at Koka Booth Amphitheatre in Cary. The program includes Mozart’s Overture to La Clemenza di Tito, Violin Concerto No. 3, and Symphony No. 41, "Jupiter."

events.name: NC GOT NEXT
events.tagline: A Raleigh weekend event at The Portal HQ.
events.description: NC GOT NEXT is listed for Raleigh at The Portal HQ for the weekend of June 19, 2026. The available listing does not provide additional public details about the program, schedule, or admission.

events.name: NCMA Movie Night: Shrek
events.tagline: Free outdoor movie night with themed programming and food trucks in downtown Raleigh.
events.description: NCMA and Moore Square present a free outdoor screening of Shrek in downtown Raleigh. The event includes themed programming starting at 6 p.m., food trucks, and the film beginning at 7:15 p.m.

events.name: Park Tour: Art and Sculpture
events.tagline: Explore NCMA’s outdoor sculpture park on a guided walking tour.
events.description: A guided walking tour of the North Carolina Museum of Art’s park focuses on the sculpture collection and other outdoor art installations. The event is listed as free and scheduled for Saturday morning, June 20, 2026.

events.name: Carolina Kitty Con
events.tagline: A cat-loving weekend of pop culture, rescue advocacy, and entertainment.
events.description: Carolina Kitty Con is a two-day feline-themed festival at the North Carolina State Fairgrounds in Raleigh. The event features entertainers, presenters, vendors, and local cat rescues.

events.name: 2026 Raleigh Hoop Nights Kick Off Event
events.tagline: Kick off Raleigh’s summer basketball series with music, community fun, and hoops.
events.description: This is the official kickoff for the 2026 Raleigh Hoop Nights season, a community basketball event hosted by the City of Raleigh and Raleigh Police Department. It takes place at Roberts Park on Saturday, June 20, from 3 to 6 PM and is free and open to youth and families.

events.name: Speed House Open Garage
events.tagline: A monthly open garage with cars, community, and culture.
events.description: Speed House’s Open Garage is a recurring Saturday morning gathering for neighbors, enthusiasts, collectors, and the car-curious. The June 2026 listing shows the event at 10:00 AM at Speed House in Durham.

events.name: Solos Duos Quartet Live Music
events.tagline: An adventurous night of solo, duo, and quartet performances at The ArtsCenter.
events.description: Concert at The ArtsCenter featuring Daniel Levin, Billy J. Hawkains III, Laurent Estoppey, and Matthew Rock. The event is scheduled for Saturday, June 20, 2026 at 7:30 PM.

events.name: Muddy Paws Hike
events.tagline: Get out on the trails with your pup for a nature walk along the Eno River.
events.venue: West Point on the Eno, Environmental Education Pavilion
events.description: Join a nature walk along the Eno River where you and your dog can meet new friends and cool off in the water. Dogs must be on a non-retractable leash, and participant registration is required.

events.name: Outdoor Pilates on the "Lawn" — A Juneteenth Wellness Experience
events.tagline: A gentle Juneteenth morning of movement, mindfulness, and community on the Historic Russell School lawn.
events.venue: Lawn of the Historic Russell School
events.description: A Juneteenth-themed outdoor Pilates class held on the lawn of the Historic Russell School in Hillsborough. The event runs from 8:00 to 9:00 AM and includes a rain date of June 27, 2026.

events.name: 9th Anniversary + Summer Solstice Wine Party
events.tagline: Celebrate Botanist & Barrel’s 9th anniversary with wine, music, and local food under the summer sky.
events.description: Botanist & Barrel is hosting a 9th Anniversary + Summer Solstice Wine Party in Cedar Grove with wine, music, and local food. The event listing indicates a Saturday start at 4 PM.

events.name: Art Resource Collective: Building a Sustainable Creative Life
events.tagline: A free Durham gathering helping artists build a more sustainable creative practice.
events.description: Building a Sustainable Creative Life is a free, community-centered gathering in Durham designed for artists and creatives. It takes place at East Regional Library and focuses on resources for building a sustainable creative life.

events.name: Mystic Father's Day Picnic & Tournament of Dads
events.tagline: A family-friendly Father’s Day picnic with contests, BBQ, and bourbon tasting at Mystic Farm & Distillery.
events.description: Mystic Farm & Distillery hosts a Father’s Day picnic featuring a dad pie-eating contest, beer-chugging contest, best dad joke contest, lawn games, BBQ, cocktails, wine, beer, soda, and a free bourbon tasting. Tickets include one free cocktail and bourbon tasting, and guests age 20 and under do not need a ticket.

events.name: ADF Presents: Camille A. Brown & Dancers w/ Post Performance Discussion
events.tagline: A high-energy ADF dance performance followed by a post-show discussion.
events.description: Camille A. Brown & Dancers appears at the American Dance Festival at Reynolds Industries Theater at Duke. The Saturday matinee includes a post-performance discussion and runs about 60 minutes.

events.name: Mean Habit / Razadan / COGS
events.tagline: A Saturday night of queer hardcore-tinged punk at The Pinhook.
events.description: Mean Habit, Razadan, and COGS are scheduled to play The Pinhook in Durham on Saturday, June 20, 2026. The listing shows it as an all-ages show with doors at 6:00 PM and music at 6:30 PM.

events.name: Street Fruit Drag & Dance Party
events.tagline: A Pride Month drag show and dance party at The Pinhook.
events.description: Street Fruit Drag + Dance Party is a Pride Month drag show and dance party at The Pinhook in Durham. Venue listings show doors at 10:00 PM and showtime at 10:30 PM on Saturday, June 20, 2026.

events.name: Peak City Band - Beer Garden Concert Series
events.tagline: Live blues-rock, local food trucks, and beer garden vibes at The Glass Jug RTP.
events.venue: The Glass Jug Beer Lab - RTP
events.description: Peak City Band performs as part of The Glass Jug Beer Lab - RTP's Beer Garden Concert Series. The event is listed for Saturday, June 20, 2026, from 6:00 PM to 9:00 PM and admission is free.

events.name: Open Stu: Juneteenth Edition
events.tagline: A free Juneteenth portrait-and-community gathering at Hayti Heritage Center.
events.description: Open Stu: Juneteenth Edition is a living documentary project focused on Black creative community, with portrait sessions, curated archival ephemera, and DJ music. The event is free and open to all at Hayti Heritage Center in Durham.

events.name: Pride Drag Show
events.tagline: A glittery Pride celebration with three drag performers and drinks at Glass Jug Downtown.
events.freeNote: Tickets $10 in advance, $20 at the door
events.description: Glass Jug Downtown’s Pride Drag Show features three performers and starts at 7:00 PM on Saturday, June 20. The event is 18+ and takes place at The Glass Jug Beer Lab in downtown Durham.

events.name: What is Architecture? Family Program
events.tagline: A hands-on family introduction to architecture, design, and building.
events.description: An in-person family event at Chapel Hill Public Library featuring architecture-themed readings and hands-on build activities for children and adults. The Eventbrite listing describes it as a two-hour program with design and construction activities.

events.name: Hillsborough Vintage Market
events.tagline: Dig through vintage treasures this Saturday from 11–4 at The Rookery!
events.description: The Hillsborough Vintage Market returns this Saturday with 30+ vendors offering vintage clothing, accessories, and home goods at The Rookery in Hillsborough.

events.name: Sweet Mama Stringbean: The Life & Times of Ethel Waters
events.tagline: A one-woman musical celebration of Ethel Waters’ life and legacy.
events.description: Agape Theatre Project presents this play about blues singer and actress Ethel Waters, with Durham performances at NCCU University Theatre. Listings indicate a weekend run in June 2026, including Saturday at 4:00 PM and Sunday at 3:00 PM.

events.name: Chrissy Chlapecka
events.tagline: Bubblegum-pop energy hits Stanczyks for a Saturday night set.
events.description: Chrissy Chlapecka is scheduled to perform at Stanczyks in Durham on Saturday, June 20, 2026. Venue listings show doors at 7:00 PM and the show at 8:00 PM.

events.name: Debí Tirar Más Fiestas | The Bad Bunny Party at Motorco
events.tagline: A 21+ Bad Bunny dance party with reggaeton, dembow, merengue, and Latin hits.
events.description: NDVST presents a 21+ dance party at Motorco Music Hall celebrating reggaeton, dembow, merengue, and other Latin dance hits. The event is scheduled for 9:00 PM with $15 admission.

events.name: Get Out of My Dreams — Sapphic Dream Club Pride Party
events.tagline: A sapphic Pride dance party at Arcana with dreamy club energy.
events.description: A Pride-themed sapphic dream club dance party at Arcana Durham on Saturday, June 20, 2026. Event listings show it starts at 9:00 PM and runs late into the night.

events.name: BTR and V&V present: Hell & Highwater Tour
events.tagline: A heavy-music night lands in Raleigh with a stacked bill at Cannonball Music Hall.
events.description: This Saturday event at Cannonball Music Hall features Hellheart and Seaside Massacre, with support from Alpha Andromeda, Absent Colors, and Celaris. Doors open at 6 PM and music starts at 7 PM; the show is all ages with a $5 surcharge for under 21.

events.name: Document One
events.tagline: Bass-heavy electronic night at Kings.
events.description: Document One is scheduled at Kings in downtown Raleigh on Saturday, June 20, 2026, at 9:00 PM. The listing shows support from ND-Skyz and Back2Wax, with admission priced at $27.50.

events.name: Jeszcze Raz: The Wolfpack Sound Band Returns
events.tagline: Free polka at Slim's Dive Bar with Raleigh's Wolfpack Sound.
events.description: A free Saturday night performance by The Wolfpack Sound Polka Band at Slim's Dive Bar in downtown Raleigh. The event is listed for June 20, 2026, from 7 to 11 PM.

events.name: Tank and the Bangas Host a Carrboro Juneteenth Celebration
events.tagline: Tank and the Bangas bring a Juneteenth weekend concert to Cat's Cradle Back Yard.
events.description: This Juneteenth weekend concert features Tank and the Bangas with opening acts ¡Tumbao! and Ariel J at Cat's Cradle Back Yard. The event is listed for Saturday, June 20, 2026, as part of Carrboro's Juneteenth celebration.

events.name: Juneteenth, Freedom in the Park
events.tagline: Celebrate Juneteenth with live performances, family activities, food, and local businesses at Mill Bridge Nature Park.
events.description: A Juneteenth celebration at Mill Bridge Nature Park with live performances, educational information, family activities, great food, and local businesses. The event is scheduled for Saturday, June 20, 2026, from 2:00 PM to 6:00 PM.

events.name: Landslide - A Tribute to Fleetwood Mac
events.tagline: A high-energy Fleetwood Mac tribute concert at Lincoln Theatre.
events.description: Landslide is a professional Fleetwood Mac tribute band performing at Lincoln Theatre in Raleigh on Saturday, June 20, 2026. The show is scheduled for 8 PM, with doors opening at 7 PM.

events.name: Left Cross w/ Serpentshrine, Vampyric Sword, Nganga.
events.tagline: A 21+ night of crushing metal at The Pour House.
events.venue: The Pour House Music Hall & Record Shop
events.description: Left Cross headlines this 21+ show at The Pour House Music Hall & Record Shop in Raleigh, joined by Serpentshrine, Vampyric Sword, and Nganga. The event is scheduled for Saturday, June 20, 2026 at 8:00 PM.

events.name: Queer Prom
events.tagline: A late-night queer prom with dancing, photo ops, and Pride-energy vibes.
events.description: Bitter Social is hosting Queer Prom on Saturday, June 20, 2026, with music by DJ Gay Agenda. The event runs from 9 PM to 2 AM at the upstairs venue above Centro in downtown Raleigh.

events.name: Queer Wrath Returns!
events.tagline: Pride meets metal in a night of drag, rage, and revolution.
events.address: 658 Maywood Ave., Ste. B 110, Raleigh, NC 27603
events.description: Queer Wrath Returns! is a vibrant performance showcase featuring drag, metal, and queer art that blends protest with celebration at Chapel of Bones.

events.name: Artet Jazz Night
events.tagline: Groove-based, partly improvised jazz night with original tunes and unexpected covers at Rebus Works.
events.description: Artet Jazz Night is a weekly event featuring groove-based, partly improvised jazz with original tunes and unexpected covers, sponsored by ARTET infused aperitifs.

events.name: Garner Juneteenth Celebration
events.tagline: A free community Juneteenth program with music, dance, and local history in Garner.
events.description: The Town of Garner’s annual Juneteenth celebration takes place at the Garner Performing Arts Center on Saturday, June 20, 2026. The program features local choirs, dance performances, storytelling, and historical reflections on Black landowners in Garner.

events.name: Raleigh Twilight Market at Smoky Hollow
events.tagline: A free monthly night market with local vendors, live music, food, and drinks.
events.description: The MAKRS Society hosts this monthly Twilight Market at Smoky Hollow on the 3rd Saturday of each month. The event features 30+ local vendors, a live DJ, performances, and food and drinks.

events.name: Triangle Pop Up – Maker’s Market
events.tagline: The cutest market in Cary happening right outside your apartment with live music, food trucks, and local shopping.
events.description: A monthly maker's market at Fenton featuring live music, food trucks, and a new selection of local vendors for shopping and community gathering.

events.name: Raleigh Food Truck Rodeo
events.tagline: Downtown Raleigh fills with local food trucks for a free summer rodeo.
events.description: Shop Local Raleigh hosts the Raleigh Food Truck Rodeo on Fayetteville Street in downtown Raleigh. The event features local food trucks and vendors, with the 2026 June date listed as Saturday, June 20, from 12 to 6 p.m.

events.name: Wienerfest
events.tagline: Motorco’s annual hot dog party returns for an afternoon of build-your-own bites and patio fun.
events.description: Wienerfest 2026 takes place on Sunday, June 21 from 4–7 PM at Motorco Music Hall’s Veranda in Durham. The event features a build-your-own hot dog celebration and is listed as ticketed.

events.name: Father's Day BBQ at Overlook
events.tagline: A laid-back Father’s Day BBQ buffet with classic favorites on the patio.
events.venue: The Overlook at Residence Inn Raleigh Downtown
events.freeNote: $50 adults; $25 children ages 5–12; free for ages 4 and under
events.description: Father’s Day BBQ at Overlook is a buffet-style patio event at The Overlook inside Residence Inn Raleigh Downtown. The menu includes BBQ ribs, baked mac & cheese, fried okra, burgers and hot dogs, sides, and dessert.

events.name: Father's Day with the Grateful Dead
events.tagline: A free Father’s Day screening celebrating the Grateful Dead.
events.description: Rialto Presents Father's Day with the Grateful Dead, a free but ticketed screening at The Rialto Theatre in Raleigh. The event is scheduled for Sunday, June 21, with doors at 6:15 pm and the show at 7:00 pm.

events.name: Father’s Day at The Willard
events.tagline: A Father’s Day rooftop celebration with views over downtown Raleigh.
events.description: Father’s Day at The Willard is listed for Sunday, June 21, 2026 at The Willard Rooftop Lounge atop the AC Hotel Raleigh Downtown. The venue is a rooftop bar and lounge in downtown Raleigh with food and drinks and city views.

events.name: Sunday Day Party at The Davie
events.tagline: A laid-back Sunday vinyl day party in downtown Raleigh.
events.description: Weekly Sunday day party at The Davie in downtown Raleigh featuring an all-vinyl DJ set. Listing sources describe it as a Sunday afternoon vibe from 2–5 PM.

events.name: Lavelle Dontae
events.tagline: Lavelle Dontae brings his live comedy set to Goodnights Comedy Club.
events.description: Lavelle Dontae is scheduled for a special event at Goodnights Comedy Club in Raleigh on Sunday, June 21, 2026. The club’s calendar shows two showtimes, 6:00 PM and 8:15 PM.

events.name: Art Cart: Father’s Day
events.tagline: Drop in for free hands-on Father’s Day crafts at Downtown Cary Park.
events.description: A family-friendly art activity at Downtown Cary Park with hands-on crafts and creative supplies for kids and caregivers. The Father’s Day edition is a free drop-in program at the park’s Great Lawn Pavilion.

events.name: Day Party Brunch
events.tagline: A Sunday brunch day party with buffet, cocktails, and live DJ energy.
events.description: Sunday Brunch Club at Botanical Lounge is an all-you-can-eat brunch buffet with craft cocktails and a lively daytime party atmosphere. It runs every Sunday from 11:00 AM to 3:00 PM at 419 Glenwood Ave in Raleigh.

events.name: Summer Family Film Series - Honey, I Shrunk the Kids
events.tagline: A retro family adventure plays at noon as part of The Cary’s summer series.
events.description: The Cary Theater is screening Honey, I Shrunk the Kids as part of its Summer Family Series. The film is rated PG and runs 93 minutes.

events.name: ADF Presents: Can You Bring It: Bill T. Jones and D-Man in the Waters w/ a Formal Talkback Session
events.tagline: A free screening of the acclaimed Bill T. Jones documentary followed by a formal talkback.
events.description: American Dance Festival presents a 90-minute screening of Can You Bring It: Bill T. Jones and D-Man in the Waters, followed by a formal talkback session. The event is part of ADF’s 30th Anniversary Screen Dance Festival.

events.name: Lip Critic
events.tagline: Noise-punk outfit Lip Critic brings its tour to The Pinhook for a late-night Durham show.
events.description: Lip Critic is scheduled to perform at The Pinhook in Durham on Sunday, June 21, 2026, with Flatwounds and Bejalvin. The show is listed for 8:00 PM with admission priced at $18.

events.name: Emo Karaoke! Get Sad Y'all
events.tagline: Sing your favorite emo songs at Neptunes' monthly Sunday karaoke night.
events.description: Emo Karaoke! Get Sad Y'all is a recurring emo karaoke night at Neptunes Parlour in downtown Raleigh. Downtown Raleigh lists it as happening every third Sunday of the month from 8 PM to midnight.

events.name: 2026 Summer Concert Series
events.tagline: Free Sunday-evening live music at Pullen Park all summer long.
events.description: A free summer concert series hosted by Raleigh Parks at Pullen Park on select Sunday evenings. For 2026, Pullen Park dates include June 21, June 28, and August 16, with performances from 6 to 8 p.m.

events.name: Domocile w/ Px3
events.tagline: A late-summer rock show at one of Raleigh’s best-known live music rooms.
events.venue: The Pour House Music Hall & Record Shop
events.description: Domocile performs with Px3 at The Pour House Music Hall & Record Shop in Raleigh on Sunday, June 21, 2026. The venue listing shows doors at 7:00 PM and showtime at 8:00 PM with tickets priced at $15.07.

events.name: Gozu / Restless Spirit / Crystal Spiders / Archaos
events.tagline: A heavy psych and stoner-metal bill lands at Chapel of Bones for a Sunday night show.
events.description: Gozu, Restless Spirit, Crystal Spiders, and Archaos are scheduled to perform at Chapel of Bones in Raleigh on Sunday, June 21, 2026 at 6:00 PM. The listing shows a ticketed admission price of $22.86.

events.name: Heated Rivalry Bingo Binge Watch Party
events.tagline: A free Sunday watch party with bingo and Heated Rivalry buzz.
events.description: A Heated Rivalry-themed bingo binge watch party at Bitter Social in downtown Raleigh. The event runs Sunday afternoon and evening with free admission.

events.name: Pride Day Party at Wolfe & Porter
events.tagline: A Pride celebration with performances, drinks, and daytime party energy in downtown Raleigh.
events.description: Wolfe & Porter is hosting a Pride Day Party in downtown Raleigh on June 21, 2026, featuring performers and an all-ages daytime portion before continuing into the evening. Listed hours run from 2 p.m. to midnight, with tickets priced at $12.51 per person.

events.name: Shadows in Red Light
events.tagline: A new musical set in the shadow of the AIDS epidemic.
events.description: Burning Coal Theatre's Second Stage production of Shadows in Red Light runs in June 2026 at Murphey School Auditorium in downtown Raleigh. The show is presented as a concert version of a new musical.

events.name: Woodstock '99 Yoga
events.tagline: A themed Sunday yoga session at Chapel of Bones.
events.description: Woodstock '99 Yoga is scheduled for Sunday, June 21, 2026 at Chapel of Bones in Raleigh. The venue lists the event at 11am, and the ticket listing shows admission priced at $21.92.

events.name: Devil Dogz vs. Tri-City Chili Peppers
events.tagline: Sunday baseball at Five County Stadium in Zebulon.
events.description: The Zebulon Devil Dogz host the Tri-City Chili Peppers at Five County Stadium on Sunday, June 21, 2026. The game is scheduled for 5:00 PM.

events.name: 2026 Premier Ultimate League Championship Weekend
events.tagline: Championship ultimate comes to Durham for a two-day title weekend.
events.description: The 2026 Premier Ultimate League Championship Weekend is scheduled for June 20-21 at Durham County Memorial Stadium in Durham, with semifinals on Saturday and the championship game on Sunday. It features women’s and gender-diverse ultimate teams competing for the league title.

events.name: Father’s Day Brunch
events.tagline: A special Sunday brunch at The Durham for the dads and dad figures in your life.
events.description: Father’s Day Brunch takes place on Sunday, June 21 at the Coffee Bar at The Durham. The menu features a three-course brunch service with family-style starters, entrée choices, and kid-friendly options.

events.name: Jambalaya Soul Slam Lyric Slam
events.tagline: Durham’s longest-running poetry event returns for a high-energy lyric slam.
events.description: The Jambalaya Soul Slam is the Triangle’s longest running poetry event. This edition is a lyric slam at Hayti Heritage Center in Durham, featuring a competitive spoken-word format centered on song lyrics.

events.name: Jennifer Curtis & Friends
events.tagline: An afternoon of live chamber music at The ArtsCenter.
events.description: Jennifer Curtis and friends perform a live concert at The ArtsCenter in Carrboro on Sunday, June 21, 2026. The event is listed as a concert with a 3:00 PM start time.
